activities.category: Old town walking & history
Canal experience
Cevichería & local food scene
Cloud forest hike with naturalist guide
Coffee farm experience
Hot springs or river day
Island hopping by private boat
Snorkel or surf day
Sloth sanctuary visit

common_mistakes: Underestimating travel time between regions — internal flights between Bocas and Panama City are essential, not optional; the overland route adds a full day each way
Visiting Bocas del Toro in October or November when the Caribbean coast gets its heaviest rains and boat transfers become rough
Booking the wrong block in Casco Viejo — three streets separates boutique-charming from actively rough; our partners know which side to be on
Skipping the Miraflores Locks visitor center and just doing a canal boat transit — for first-timers, watching from the viewing platform is often more dramatic

regions.name: Panama City
regions.stay_type: modern hotel near the old city

activities.category: Canal experience
Rainforest walk in the city's metropolitan park
Mangrove kayaking at dawn
Snorkeling in Bastimentos Marine Park
Night walk for wildlife
Quetzal trail in La Amistad park
Whitewater rafting on the Chiriquí Viejo
Coffee origin experience
Cooking class using local highland produce
Sea turtle nesting beach (seasonal)
Traditional crafts village visit

common_mistakes: Flying into Bocas on the same day you arrive in Panama City — the connection is tight and missed flights are common in rainy season
Doing the Quetzal trail without a local birding guide — the trail splits and the bird is easy to miss without trained eyes
Visiting Azuero in peak dry season without booking ahead; the hacienda-style properties fill up months in advance
Treating the Chiriquí Viejo rafting as an add-on — it deserves a full day and proper conditions

activities.category: Casco Viejo UNESCO walking tour and rooftop cocktail at dusk
Panama Canal Miraflores Locks — watching Panamax vessels pass
Panama City gastronomy — ceviche, patacones and the Casco food scene

regions.name: Bocas del Toro (including Isla Bastimentos)
regions.stay_type: Pure Roots eco-lodge on Isla Bastimentos — wellness and Caribbean immersion

activities.category: Snorkel and freedive in the Bastimentos Marine Park coral gardens
Surf session at Playa Bluff or Silverbacks (intermediate to advanced)
Mangrove kayak at dawn — manatees and pink dolphins occasionally visible
Bocas Town night — Afro-Caribbean food and reggaeton waterfront bars

regions.name: Boquete (Chiriquí highlands)
regions.stay_type: mountain lodge or coffee finca stay

activities.category: Geisha coffee origin experience at one of Panama's most awarded fincas
Quetzal trail birding walk in La Amistad biosphere reserve
Volcán Barú summit hike at night — arriving for a both-ocean sunrise (strenuous)

regions.name: Valle de Antón
regions.stay_type: boutique hotel inside the extinct volcano crater valley

activities.category: Crater rim hike — panoramic views of the caldera from above
Thermal hot springs and mineral pools on the crater floor
Canopy zip-line through the cloud forest above the valley

common_mistakes: Flying between Bocas and Panama City without checking small-plane schedules early — flights sell out in high season and the overland route adds a full day
Visiting Bocas in October–November when Caribbean swells turn boat transfers rough and rain is near-constant
Attempting Volcán Barú without proper cold-weather gear — summit temperatures drop below 5°C and the trail starts at midnight
Booking generic Boquete coffee tours instead of arranging finca visits through a local contact — the top producers are small-batch and require advance reservation
